response:
MOORE
SOME EXPERTS SPECULATE THAT MOORE'S LAW WILL CEASE TO HOLD TRUE AROUND THE 2020S OR EARLY 2030S DUE TO PHYSICAL LIMITATIONS IN TRANSISTOR SCALING AND THERMAL DISSIPATION ISSUES. AS SILICON TECHNOLOGY APPROACHES ATOMIC SIZES, THE TRADITIONAL PREDICTION OF DOUBLING PROCESSING POWER EVERY TWO YEARS BECOMES INCREASINGLY UNFEASIBLE WITHOUT RADICAL INNOVATIONS IN MATERIALS AND ARCHITECTURES.
***
ALTERNATIVE APPROACHES SUCH AS THREE-DIMENSIONAL STACKING, QUANTUM COMPUTING, AND SPECIALIZED HARDWARE ARE EXPECTED TO REPLACE THE LINEAR GROWTH MODEL WITH MORE SUSTAINABLE METHODS OF PERFORMANCE ENHANCEMENT. WHILE THE STRICT DEFINITION OF MOORE'S LAW MAY FADE, THE UNDERLYING DRIVE FOR COMPUTATIONAL ADVANCEMENT WILL CONTINUE THROUGH THESE NEW PARADIGMS THAT DEFY CONVENTIONAL PHYSICS.
MOORE
